course_description:
This course aims to introduce students to the basic skills of consecutive interpreting. Students will study the preparation, writing and delivery of short, set-time speeches on a wide variety of topics, thus familiarising them with the type of language and set expressions they may encounter when working as interpreters. Emphasis will be placed on content, structure, delivery and non-verbal skills. Note-taking will be introduced so students can learn to take effective notes in actual interpreting situations.

This course starts with public speaking and consecutive interpreting of typical beginnings, endings and set expressions in formal speeches, and then focuses on dialogue interpreting practice in community settings in Australia.

learning_outcomes.description: be equipped with the English and LOTE language skills required for a public speaking situation and prepare and effectively deliver short speeches in English and LOTE for a variety of scenarios with minimum reference to notes.
use basic skills in performing dialogue interpreting on non-technical topics and skilfully manage the interpreting situation, including in basic remote dialogue interpreting settings.
take effective notes to aid consecutive interpreting on general topics.

assessment_summary.title: Consecutive Interpreting of Short Speech Segments

assessment_details.title: Consecutive Interpreting of Short Speech Segments
assessment_details.task_description: Students will interpret very short speech segments from English into LOTE (Chinese/Japanese) and from LOTE (Chinese/Japanese) into English in the consecutive mode. Four short speech segments (2 in English and 2 in LOTE) are taken out of formal speeches in the speech categories covered in class during the first few weeks of the semester. The short speech segments may be the beginnings, endings or set expressions in formal speeches. Students will be given the speech topics and interpreting brief one week in advance. On the day of the exam, there won't be any preparation time for each interpreting activity. One A4 sheet of handwritten or typed notes, double sided, is permitted. Students' interpreting performance in the exam will be assessed according to the marking rubric of this course. Statement on Generative AI & MT This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.exam_details: Planning time no planning time minutes Duration 60 minutes Calculator options No calculators permitted Open/closed book Closed book examination - specified written materials permitted Materials One A4 sheet of handwritten or typed notes, double sided, is permitted Blank notepads for taking brief notes during consecutive interpreting also permitted. Exam platform Paper based Invigilation Invigilated in person
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
This assessment task is designed to be face-to-face in a designated lab. Students' interpreting performances are recorded by the lecturer using voice-recorders in the designated lab for this course. Students can use their own voice-recorders to record their interpreting performances as a backup in case there is a technical problem.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to defer this exam .
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
Students need to upload their backup recording of their interpreting performance to a dedicated section in Assessment on Blackboard Ultra within two hours of the end of the class. If not, their submission is considered 'late submission'.

assessment_details.title: In-semester Dialogue Interpreting Exam
assessment_details.task_description: Students will be required to interpret a dialogue in the consecutive mode. The dialogue will be played from an audio recording. One week prior to the test, students will receive the topic for the dialogue interpreting task. On the day of the exam, students will not have any preparation time for the interpreting activity. One A4 sheet of handwritten or typed notes, double sided, is permitted. Students' interpreting performance in the exam will be assessed according to the marking rubric of this course. Statement on Generative AI & MT This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. learning_resources.library_resources: Library resources are available on the UQ Library website .

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 2
learning_activities.topic: Public speaking lecture and consecutive interpreting practice One-hour lecture on public speaking and associated knowledge in week 1. Half an hour of shadowing of formal speeches in English and LOTE in week 1. One-and-a-half-hour practice of consecutive interpreting of short speech segments in week 1. Three-hour practice of consecutive interpreting of short speech segments in week 2. Learning outcomes: L01

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 3 To Week 5
learning_activities.topic: Dialogue interpreting practice (Chinese stream and Japanese stream are combined) There will be a lecture on dialogue interpreting in week 3. Students will then be divided into three-person groups to engage in role-plays in community settings, in which one person will act as an English-speaking professional, another person will act as a LOTE (Chinese/Japanese)-speaking client, and the other person will work as an interpreter facilitating the communication between those two primary interlocutors. Scripts containing English and LOTE will be provided to the students who act as the primary interlocutors. Students will then participate in discussions regarding the quality of interpreting performance and interpreters' interactional management. Learning outcomes: L02, L03

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 6 To Week 11
learning_activities.topic: Dialogue interpreting practice (language-specific tutorials) Weekly 3-hour language specific tutorial for dialogue interpreting practice, with teacher feedback on the quality of interpreting performance. Students will listen to dialogues in the form of voice-recordings and practice dialogue interpreting. Students may take brief notes during the listening comprehension phase to aid the dialogue interpreting. Learning outcomes: L02, L03

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 12 To Week 13 (20 Oct - 02 Nov)
learning_activities.topic: Dialogue interpreting practice (Chinese stream and Japanese stream are combined) Students will be divided into three-person groups to engage in role-plays in community settings, in which one person will act as an English-speaking professional, another person will act as a LOTE (Chinese/Japanese)-speaking client, and the other person will work as an interpreter facilitating the communication between those two primary interlocutors. Scripts containing English and LOTE will be provided to the students who act as the primary interlocutors. Students will then participate in discussions regarding the quality of interpreting performance and interpreters' interactional management. Learning outcomes: L02, L03
